topic.what: Dynamic-static LLM-assisted greybox fuzzing for structured-input programs
topic.how: Combines LLM-based structure-aware seed generation with static crash analysis in a unified feedback loop
topic.why_matters: Traditional fuzzing fails on structured inputs due to strict syntactic constraints; existing LLM-assisted approaches underutilize runtime feedback

characteristics.what_it_measures: Number of real vulnerabilities triggered in structured-input targets
characteristics.vs_baseline: outperforms AFL++ and LLM Seed + AFL++
characteristics.evidence: Figure 2 and Figure 3 show higher median and average bug counts with lower variance across 24h and 48h evaluations
characteristics.context: Evaluated on libxml2, libpng, libsndfile from Magma benchmark

characteristics.what_it_measures: Time required to discover each vulnerability
characteristics.vs_baseline: faster convergence than AFL++ and LLM Seed + AFL++
characteristics.evidence: Figure 4 heatmap and Figure 5 curves show darker colors indicating shorter discovery times
Described as evaluation metric; SDLLMFuzz maintains advantage across extended time budgets
characteristics.context: Affects exploration of deeper execution paths

characteristics.what_it_measures: Computational cost of LLM integration and crash analysis
characteristics.evidence: Section 6.3 acknowledges additional computational overhead from model inference and crash analysis
characteristics.context: LLM inference and GDB-based static analysis add latency

characteristics.vs_baseline: SDLLMFuzz maintains advantage at both time budgets
characteristics.evidence: Comparing Figures 2 and 3: all methods improve but with limited gains

not_for: real-time systems with strict latency requirements
stateful systems requiring complex state modeling
requires: LLM API or local model access
structured input programs with syntactic constraints

novelty: Dynamic-static feedback loop integrating LLM generation with crash artifact analysis
Crash feedback encoding transforming core dumps into semantic guidance for LLM prompts
Structure-aware seed generation leveraging LLM semantic understanding for syntactic constraints
Coverage-aware seed selection scoring mechanism combining edge and block coverage
open_problems: Improving LLM integration efficiency to reduce computational overhead
Exploring advanced feedback mechanisms beyond crash analysis
Extending framework to protocol fuzzing and stateful systems
Validating generalization to diverse real-world software beyond Magma benchmark
